tasks.title: project_manager: Multi-project priority ranking in PM (BL-004, S-003-1)

mvp_template.doel: Add scripts/pm-priority.py that reads queue.json and outputs a ranked markdown table of all tasks sorted by the PM multi-project priority order: paused first, then project_manager tasks, then P1>P2>P3, then oldest created date.
niet_in_scope: New priority tiers beyond existing P1/P2/P3
acceptatiecriteria: scripts/pm-priority.py reads tasks/queue.json and outputs a ranked markdown table (rank, task-id, project, priority, status, title)
Paused tasks always appear first regardless of project or priority
project_manager tasks outrank same-priority tasks from other projects
Within same project+priority, oldest created date wins
/pm-status skill updated to invoke pm-priority.py
mvp_template.security_arch_impact: None — reads local files only; no outbound HTTP; no path construction
mvp_template.tests_required: Unit — fixture queue.json with mixed projects/priorities/statuses; assert output order matches ranking rules for each scenario
mvp_template.rollback_plan: Delete scripts/pm-priority.py; revert pm-status.md edit — Michel Maas

tasks.title: project_manager: Python token-cap-enforcer script (BL-050, S-003-4)

mvp_template.doel: Implement scripts/token_cap_enforcer.py that reads a task's token_estimate from queue.json by task_id and exits with a structured error if it exceeds 400,000 (80% of the 500k project cap).
acceptatiecriteria: Script accepts --task-id argument; reads tasks/queue.json
If token_estimate > 400000: prints 'ALERT: Task <id> estimated tokens (<n>) exceed 80% of project cap (400k)' and exits code 1
If token_estimate <= 400000: prints 'OK: <id> token_estimate=<n>' and exits 0
manager.yaml step 5 references scripts/token_cap_enforcer.py explicitly by path
mvp_template.security_arch_impact: None — reads queue.json only; no outbound; no path construction
mvp_template.tests_required: Unit — fixture queue.json with tasks above and below threshold; assert exit code and output message for each case
definition_of_done: manager.yaml step 5 updated to name script explicitly
mvp_template.rollback_plan: Delete scripts/token_cap_enforcer.py; revert manager.yaml — Michel Maas

tasks.title: project_manager: Cross-project kanban view script (BL-064, S-003-3)

mvp_template.doel: Implement scripts/cross-kanban.py that generates a unified markdown kanban across all managed projects by reading queue.json and docs/project-registry.md, grouped by project then by status column.
acceptatiecriteria: Script outputs markdown with one section per project, each containing a kanban table (status | task-id | title) for that project's tasks
Projects with no active tasks are omitted from output
Output callable from /pm-status (one line added to pm-status.md)
Script handles queue.json with 0 tasks gracefully (prints 'No active tasks')
mvp_template.security_arch_impact: None — reads local files only; no outbound HTTP; no path construction
mvp_template.tests_required: Unit — fixture queue.json with tasks across 3 projects; assert each project section present, correct tasks in correct status columns
mvp_template.rollback_plan: Delete scripts/cross-kanban.py; revert pm-status.md — Michel Maas

tasks.title: mas_agent: Fix mas-frontend Docker healthcheck (BL-059)
notes: Root cause confirmed: BusyBox wget resolves localhost to [::1] (IPv6), nginx listens on 0.0.0.0:80 (IPv4) only. Fix: change healthcheck to 127.0.0.1

mvp_template.doel: Fix the mas-frontend Docker healthcheck by changing `localhost` to `127.0.0.1` in docker-compose.yml (or Dockerfile), resolving the IPv6/IPv4 mismatch that caused 82,000+ consecutive false-unhealthy reports over 4+ months.
acceptatiecriteria: docker-compose.yml healthcheck directive uses 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost
After docker-compose up -d --build mas-frontend on Pi4: docker ps shows mas-frontend as healthy
Consecutive healthcheck failure count resets to 0
Commit pushed to micmaas2/mas_personal_assistant repo
mvp_template.security_arch_impact: None — change confined to healthcheck directive; no outbound HTTP beyond localhost
mvp_template.tests_required: Post-deploy: ssh pi4 'docker inspect mas-frontend --format={{.State.Health.Status}}' returns healthy; docker ps shows (healthy)
definition_of_done: artefacts/task-018/verification.md contains PASS + docker ps output
mvp_template.rollback_plan: Revert healthcheck line in docker-compose.yml, rebuild container — Michel Maas

tasks.title: mas_agent: Telegram sender chat_id auth guard (BL-060)
notes: Security gap: any Telegram user who discovers the bot token can send commands and receive responses

mvp_template.doel: Add inbound chat_id validation to telegram_listener.py in mas_personal_assistant — drop and log any Telegram message whose chat_id does not match settings.telegram_chat_id.
acceptatiecriteria: At top of _process_update(), compare message['chat']['id'] (int) against settings.telegram_chat_id
Unauthorized chat_id: log WARNING 'Unauthorized chat_id <id> — dropping' and return without processing
Authorized chat_id: processed exactly as before (no behaviour change)
Unit test with authorized + unauthorized chat_id fixture payloads; both cases assert correctly
settings.telegram_chat_id verified present in .env.production before Builder runs
mvp_template.security_arch_impact: Closes active P1 security gap — restricts bot to a single authorised chat_id; settings.telegram_chat_id must be set in .env.production (Builder must verify before proceeding)
mvp_template.tests_required: Unit — mock _process_update with authorized and unauthorized chat_id payloads; assert authorized → processed, unauthorized → dropped + WARNING logged
definition_of_done: Verified via ssh pi4 docker logs mas-telegram shows no unauthorized processing
mvp_template.rollback_plan: Revert telegram_listener.py change and redeploy — Michel Maas
mvp_template.privacy_dpia: No — chat_id is a non-personal Telegram identifier

Projects not listed (CCAS, genealogie, performance_HPT, pi-homelab hardening)
Cloud infrastructure or provider migration decisions

Improvement proposals ranked P1/P2/P3 by effort-vs-impact; minimum 3 per component
Each proposal cross-references an existing BL item or proposes a new BL-NNN entry
Reviewer APPROVED; no unresolved open questions in the document
mvp_template.security_arch_impact: None — read-only analysis; no code written, no external API calls required. If Architect reads Pi4 state via SSH: read-only commands only (no writes).

tasks.title: pensieve: Activate Gmail capture workflow in n8n

acceptatiecriteria: n8n workflow JSON produced and importable on Pi4 without SQLITE_CONSTRAINT errors
Workflow triggers on Gmail label or sender filter (configurable via env var or n8n credential)
Email body/subject parsed and passed to Claude API (Haiku) for summary + tags + topic
Output written to vault as YAML frontmatter note matching current template (source:email, date, title, summary, key_points, tags, topic)
Manual import test on Pi4 documented in artefacts/task-009/deploy-notes.md; workflow JSON validates via python3 -m json.tool

tasks.title: pensieve: Retroactive vault quality improvement script

mvp_template.doel: Build a Python script that retroactively improves existing Obsidian vault notes: migrates old-format notes to YAML frontmatter, normalises tags, re-enriches non-LinkedIn URL captures via Claude API, and flags unresolvable captures with a needs-review tag.
niet_in_scope: modifying the folder structure of existing notes (covered by task-004 going forward)
acceptatiecriteria: Migrates old-format notes (# heading + **bold** fields + #hashtag tags) to YAML frontmatter matching the current template
Normalises concatenated tags to hyphenated lowercase (e.g. aiagents → ai-agents)
For source:link notes with non-LinkedIn URLs: fetches page content (requests + BeautifulSoup), re-calls Claude API with improved prompt, rewrites summary/key_points/tags
For LinkedIn or unresolvable URLs: adds needs-review tag without modifying existing content
Supports --dry-run flag that prints planned changes without writing files
Passes pylint / flake8 with no errors
Script runs on Pi 4 via: ssh pi4 python3 /tmp/migrate-vault.py --vault /opt/obsidian-vault [--dry-run]
mvp_template.security_arch_impact: Low — reads/writes local vault files; Claude API key passed via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var, never hardcoded; URL fetching uses requests with 10s timeout and no redirect to private IPs
mvp_template.tests_required: Unit tests with fixture notes covering: old-format migration, tag normalisation, LinkedIn detection, dry-run mode
definition_of_done: test_report.md contains PASS with >=90% pass rate
mvp_template.rollback_plan: Script makes in-place edits — run with --dry-run first; vault is synced to OneDrive so files can be restored from OneDrive version history if needed
mvp_template.privacy_dpia: No — notes are personal knowledge captures, no third-party personal data
mvp_template.cost_estimate: EUR 0.01 (Claude API calls for URL re-enrichment of ~5-10 existing URL notes)

Parse Claude Response node validates new fields (analysis string, topic slug format)
workflow JSON validates: python3 -m json.tool workflows/telegram-capture.json
prompts/claude-processor.txt and templates/note-template.md updated to reflect new schema
